expected_properties: overflow = 1 when positive overflow condition occurs (A positive, B negative, result negative)
additional_cases.goal: Confirm positive overflow detection for signed subtraction
notes: Uses maximum positive operand and a negative operand to trigger overflow
stimulus_intent: Apply A = 0x7FFFFFFFFFFFFFFF, B = 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF (i.e., -1) to cause positive overflow
timing_assumptions: Result and overflow become valid after an unknown latency once inputs are stable

expected_properties: Each subtraction produces correct result and overflow independently of previous operation
additional_cases.goal: Validate module handles consecutive subtractions without intermediate reset
notes: Applies two distinct operand pairs sequentially
stimulus_intent: First apply A1,B1, observe result/overflow, then change to A2,B2 and observe new result/overflow
timing_assumptions: Each operation is allowed to complete before the next input change; latency is unknown

baseline_case_enrichments.goal: Validate functional subtraction for arbitrary 64‑bit values
notes: Uses deterministic random stimulus within contract‑defined ports
stimulus_intent: Provide random 64‑bit values for A and B
timing_assumptions: Observe result and overflow after an unknown latency once inputs are stable

expected_properties: Overflow flag correctly reflects sign overflow conditions
notes: Covers max positive, most negative, and zero‑like patterns
stimulus_intent: Apply max/min signed values and zero‑like patterns on A and B
timing_assumptions: Do not assume fixed latency; use reset‑safe or protocol‑safe observation windows
